mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation expansion and faster risk-taking, so a downward move in policy rates predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if the sign of the relation flips in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and imminent earnings downgrades, which should pressure equities lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment jumps are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time or if the average forward return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger final demand and housing-linked consumption, which supports corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in retail sales and housing starts fail to improve 60-day forward SPX returns relative to either series improving alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is still elevated, the market reads the data as stronger real activity without immediate inflation relief, supporting margins and reducing recession odds, which should boost near-term equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL remains above its 6-month average while INDPRO posts a monthly increase
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-up/CPI-high condition does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level tightens discount rates and corporate financing conditions, so when policy remains restrictive equities should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward 20-day returns are not negative on average when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if high-rate months show no deterioration versus low-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior expansion signals an incipient output slowdown and weaker future earnings, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts from the prior quarterly print
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than baseline or if the average forward return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumption and earnings expectations, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls over the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll gains and unemployment declines fail to outperform single-series labor signals or if SPX 20-day returns are not positive in triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a rate-cutting environment, a simultaneous drop in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL confirms disinflation with easing policy, which should compress real yields and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is falling while CPIAUCSL is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined easing-plus-disinflation condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not predominantly positive in triggered periods.
